hypothesis: Incorporating multi-modal pretraining that jointly trains on SEM images paired with their corresponding electrochemical impedance spectroscopy signatures from additive-free samples will yield superior cross-domain transfer to additive-containing target domains compared to SEM-only pretraining, because the electrochemical features encode degradation-state information orthogonal to morphological appearance, thereby providing the backbone with physically grounded representations that remain invariant to additive-induced surface texture changes and reducing the fine-tuning data requirements by at least 50% while maintaining equivalent classification accuracy.
